title: Educational investment
description: Practice your understanding of relationship between education and earnings.

assessment_items.question_content:
**When making an *educational investment*, what is it that you are actually investing?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: your time, money, and effort into getting a higher education
your money into buying more books
your time volunteering at a community college

assessment_items.question_content:
**When making an *educational investment*, what is it that you are actually doing?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: getting higher education to improve skills, knowledge, and access to better job opportunities
investing in a savings account with high interest rates
starting a small business selling educational materials

assessment_items.question_content:
**When making an *educational investment*, what is it that you are actually doing?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: enrolling in higher education that leads to better skills, knowledge, and job opportunities
teaching others how to invest their money
buying stocks in education companies

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is  a chart from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Which educational level has the *highest* weekly pay?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A bar graph shows the horizontal axis labeled Median weekly earnings in dollars and the vertical axis that lists levels of education, starting at Less than high school to Doctoral degree. The horizontal axis is labeled, from left to right: 0, 1000, and 2000. The vertical axis is labeled from the bottom of the axis to the top of the axis as follows: Less than high school diploma, High school diploma, Some college, Associate's degree, Bachelor's degree, Master's degree, Professional degree, and Doctoral degree. The bar line for each level of education shows the following: Less than high school diploma extends to 626, High school diploma extends to 809, Some college extends to 899, Associate's degree extends to 963, Bachelor's degree extends to 1334, Master's degree extends to 1574, Professional degree extends to 1924, and Doctoral degree extends to 1909.
options.caption: Median weekly earnings by education. *Source of data: [U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics](https://www.bls.gov/emp/chart-unemployment-earnings-education.htm)*

choices.content: doctoral degree
professional degree
master's degree

Below is  a chart from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Which educational level has the *lowest* weekly pay?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A bar graph shows the horizontal axis labeled Median weekly earnings in dollars and the vertical axis that lists levels of education, starting at Less than high school to Doctoral degree. The horizontal axis is labeled, from left to right: 0, 1000, and 2000. The bar line for each level of education shows the following: Less than high school diploma extends to 626, High school diploma extends to 809, Some college extends to 899, Associate's degree extends to 963, Bachelor's degree extends to 1334, Master's degree extends to 1574, Professional degree extends to 1924, and Doctoral degree extends to 1909.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is  a chart from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Which is a true statement about a *doctoral degree*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A bar graph shows the horizontal axis labeled Median weekly earnings in dollars and the vertical axis that lists levels of education, starting at Less than high school to Doctoral degree. The horizontal axis is labeled, from left to right: 0, 1000, and 2000. The vertical axis is labeled from the bottom of the axis to the top of the axis as follows: Less than high school diploma, High school diploma, Some college, Associate's degree, Bachelor's degree, Master's degree, Professional degree, and Doctoral degree. The bar line for each level of education shows the following: Less than high school diploma extends to 626, High school diploma extends to 809, Some college extends to 899, Associate's degree extends to 963, Bachelor's degree extends to 1334, Master's degree extends to 1574, Professional degree extends to 1924, and Doctoral degree extends to 1909.
options.caption: Median weekly earnings by education. *Source of data: [U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics](https://www.bls.gov/emp/chart-unemployment-earnings-education.htm)*

choices.content: It has the *second* highest weekly pay.
It has the highest weekly pay.
It has the lowest weekly pay.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best describes the relationship between educational levels and earnings?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: People with higher education levels typically earn *less* money.
People with higher education levels typically earn *more* money.
The amount of money someone earns is only determined by their age and experience.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best describes the relationship between educational levels and earnings?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Higher education levels lead to *lower*-paying jobs.
Higher education generally results in *better* paying jobs.
The amount of money someone earns is mostly determined by company they work for.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best describes the relationship between educational levels and earnings?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The more education a person has, the *less* money they are likely to earn.
The more education a person has, the higher the pay they receive
The amount of money someone earns is mostly determined by the number of hours they work.

assessment_items.question_content:
The economy and labor markets have changed a lot in recent years.

 **As a result, which of these jobs has become more popular and higher paying?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: taxi driver
nurse
cashier

assessment_items.question_content:
The economy and labor markets have changed a lot in recent years.

 **As a result, which of these jobs has become more popular and higher paying?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: librarian
electrical engineer
fast food worker

assessment_items.question_content:
The economy and labor markets have changed a lot in recent years.
